advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Key the idempotence skip on the material fingerprint (not the claim gate's whole-HEAD freshness) so a bare HEAD advance with unchanged material does not force a re-review, per the ticket AC.
evidence: `gate = claim_gate_check(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` then `if not gate.get("ok"): return None` (__init__.py:426-428) — the skip fires ONLY when the full claim gate passes

attest.py:667-676: unscoped branch `head = signing.head_sha(...)` / `if head == "unknown" or attestation.get("head_sha") != head: return {... "verdict": "stale-head"}` — a HEAD advance invalidates even with unchanged material

Ticket AC: 'A HEAD advance that does not change the ticket's reviewed material does NOT by itself trigger a re-run (idempotence is keyed on material, not bare HEAD)'; Notes: the claim gate's head-based staleness 'is over-broad and is the root of the wasted work here'
Tests never advance HEAD between the two back-to-back reviews (test_plan_review_gate.py:1000-1017), so the material-not-HEAD requirement is uncovered

`gate = claim_gate_check(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` and `material = attest.current_material_fingerprint(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` (__init__.py:426,433) — both take raw repo_root, not the resolved SnapshotHandle
For an unscoped attestation compute_validity checks `signing.head_sha(_config.repo_root(repo_root))` (attest.py:667) — the working-tree HEAD, not the requested `ref`

_add_ref_source help documents `--ref HEAD` for 'code you have committed locally but not yet landed on the default ref' (_llm_commands.py:39-44) — a first-class scenario where basis differs from the attestation's signed SHA
advisory.finding: For an UNSCOPED plan-review attestation (no cited deps / empty file_impact), the idempotence skip inherits claim_gate_check's whole-HEAD freshness, so a bare `HEAD` advance that does NOT change the ticket's reviewed material still invalidates the attestation (`stale-head`) and forces a full LLM re-review — directly contradicting the ticket's acceptance criterion that idempotence be keyed on material, not bare HEAD.
merged_from: `_idempotent_reuse` decides reuse via `claim_gate_check`/`current_material_fingerprint` against the raw `repo_root`, ignoring the review's requested `ref`/`source`; so a review explicitly pinned to a DIFFERENT code basis (e.g. `--ref HEAD` for a stacked change, or `source=local`) can be short-circuited into reusing an attestation bound to another basis, silently returning PASS for a basis that was never reviewed.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Surface a user-facing skip notice (with the reused sha/timestamp and the --force hint) on the CLI/text path, not just logger.info.
evidence: `logger.info("plan review reused (ticket unchanged; attestation current) for %s — pass --force to re-run", ticket_id)` (__init__.py:453-457) — no sha/ts, and logger.info is not stdout

Ticket AC: 'The short-circuit prints a clear feedback message explaining the behavior and how to override (--force)'; Expected: 'prints ... "plan review skipped: ticket unchanged since last review at <sha>/<ts>; pass --force to re-run"'
CLI `_review_plan` writes only `result` to stdout (json/text); no dedicated skip notice (_llm_commands.py:450-453)
advisory.finding: The skip's feedback message omits the reviewed sha/timestamp that the acceptance criterion calls for ('plan review skipped: ... at <sha>/<ts>; pass --force to re-run'); it only emits a logger.info line (not stdout) naming --force, so a CLI user sees no user-facing explanation of why the LLM did not run.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test co-enabling progressive drift-refresh that proves a fully-valid attestation takes the idempotence skip BEFORE drift-refresh, and that --force bypasses drift-refresh too (full re-review).
evidence: Code: `if sign and not force:` (skip) precedes the `if sign and _progressive_enabled(repo_root): refreshed = orchestrator.drift_refresh(...)` block — src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:480-489

Docs: "It is ordered before the code-drift drift_refresh check below (a fully-valid attestation beats a needs-refresh one)" and "--force ... bypasses both the idempotence skip and the drift-refresh" — docs/plan-review-gate.md

ABSENCE: `search_files` for `drift_refresh` in test_plan_review_gate.py returns no match in the new b3e5 block; test_force_reruns_the_llm_on_an_unchanged_ticket only asserts the LLM ran again, not that drift-refresh was bypassed, and no test co-enables progressive drift-refresh with an unchanged-but-drifted attestation to prove the skip wins.
MCP adds `force: bool = False` threaded as `force=force` — src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py:181-217
Test invokes the library directly: `rebar.llm.review_plan(tid, runner=runner, repo_root=str(rebar_repo), force=True)` — test_plan_review_gate.py:1029 (not via _cli.main or the MCP tool)
advisory.finding: No test exercises the ordering claim that the idempotence skip fires BEFORE the drift-refresh check, nor that --force bypasses the drift-refresh path. The diff (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:480-489) and docs both assert the skip is ordered before drift_refresh and that --force bypasses BOTH; a regression reordering the checks or letting force fall through to drift-refresh would not be caught.
merged_from: The CLI --force flag and MCP force parameter wiring is untested end-to-end. The force test calls the library rebar.llm.review_plan(..., force=True) directly; no test drives argparse (rebar review-plan --force) or the MCP review_plan tool to confirm force is threaded through.
scenarios: A refactor that moves the drift_refresh block above the idempotence skip on a code-drifted-but-still-material-valid ticket
A change that lets --force fall through into the drift_refresh branch instead of a full review

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test that a sign=False review of an already-attested ticket runs the LLM (no idempotent_skip), pinning the skip to the signing path.
evidence: Code guard: `if sign and not force: reused = _idempotent_reuse(...)` — src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:480
Doc: "applies only when signing (a --no-sign / readonly review has no attestation to reuse)" — docs/plan-review-gate.md
ABSENCE: all four new tests call `_review`/`review_plan` on the default signing path; none pass sign=False to assert the skip is suppressed.
advisory.finding: No test asserts that the idempotence skip is confined to the signing path — i.e. a sign=False / readonly review does NOT short-circuit (never sets coverage.idempotent_skip). The guard is `if sign and not force`; dropping the `sign and` clause would let a readonly review spuriously reuse an attestation, undetected.
scenarios: A regression removing the `sign and` guard causes a --no-sign review to short-circuit and emit idempotent_skip
